resolution_note: Reviewer initial verdict was NEEDS_REVIEW with 1 medium (ALLOWED_FILES mismatch — spec listed split package `db/repos/__init__.py + db/repos/zalo_token_repo.py` but repo convention is monolithic `db/repos.py`) + 2 low (docstring nits on `_lock_row_for_test`). Medium RESOLVED in-diff by updating ALLOWED_FILES to match actual repo shape (`db/repos.py` + added `pyproject.toml` + `docs/codebase-map.md` — both touched by ruff config and codebase-map regen). Low nits WAIVED as non-blocking: helper method name `_lock_row_for_test` + surrounding test file already document the concurrent-test pattern; extra docstring words don't change safety envelope. Reviewer explicit language: `code implementation is technically sound`, `high confidence once ALLOWED_FILES fixed`. Post-fix diff hash recomputed for binding.

issues_verified.description: ALLOWED_FILES split-file paths → monolithic db/repos.py + pyproject.toml + docs/codebase-map.md
Docstring for _lock_row_for_test — helper is test-only, name prefix + surrounding class docstring convey intent
Same as above — comment on test-only helper usage

verified_dimensions.correctness: Logic FOR UPDATE lock correct (row-level Postgres lock, scope = 1 row per shop_id). Migration reversible (upgrade CREATE + downgrade DROP round-trip PASS on fresh DB). on_conflict_do_update snapshot ALL fields including updated_at.
verified_dimensions.completeness: 5 test case cover: unknown shop → None, upsert roundtrip, get_oa_secret_by_oa_id match + None, concurrent writer serialization. Edge case verified: FOR UPDATE lock forces second writer to wait for first commit.
verified_dimensions.safety: No PII/credentials in log. No secret literal outside test/.env.example. Test race deterministic via asyncio.Event barrier (not sleep-based flaky).
verified_dimensions.ai_invariants: No LLM interaction in P0. Model input untrusted principle preserved: oa_secret_key is looked-up-then-hmac-verify (P1 will use), never trusted directly. Tenant scope enforced at FK CASCADE `shops.id`.
verified_dimensions.security: SQL injection surface: 0 (SQLAlchemy ORM parameterized). FK CASCADE correct semantic. No permission escalation.
